title: Ricinocarpus grandidentatus
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus grandidentatus is classified within the plant family Celastraceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, is a part of the class Magnoliopsida. Within the Celastraceae family, Ricinocarpus grandidentatus is a unique species with its distinct characteristics.
uses: Ricinocarpus grandidentatus, also known as the broad-leaved ricinocarpus, has been found to have various medicinal uses. Its extracts have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
